methods.name: AreaForceShell
methods.description:
Reports the area forces for the specified area elements that are assigned 
 shell section properties (not plane or asolid properties). 
 Note that the forces reported are per unit of in-plane length.
methods.signature: int AreaForceShell( string Name, eItemTypeElm ItemTypeElm, ref int NumberResults, ref string[] Obj, ref string[] Elm, ref string[] PointElm, ref string[] LoadCase, ref string[] StepType, ref double[] StepNum, ref double[] F11, ref double[] F22, ref double[] F12, ref double[] FMax, ref double[] FMin, ref double[] FAngle, ref double[] FVM, ref double[] M11, ref double[] M22, ref double[] M12, ref double[] MMax, ref double[] MMin, ref double[] MAngle, ref double[] V13, ref double[] V23, ref double[] VMax, ref double[] VAngle )
params: Type: SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTBCB27CA5_1?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");String The name of an existing area object, area element or group of objects, depending on the value of the ItemTypeElm item

Type: ETABSv1AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTBCB27CA5_2?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");eItemTypeElm This is one of the following items in the eItemTypeElm enumeration. If this item is ObjectElm, the result request is for the area elements corresponding to the area object specified by the Name item. If this item is Element, the result request is for the area element specified by the Name item. If this item is GroupElm, the result request is for the area elements corresponding to all area objects included in the group specified by the Name item. If this item is SelectionElm, the result request is for area elements corresponding to all selected area objects and the Name item is ignored.

Type: AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTB3192730_11?cpp=array<");SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTB3192730_12?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");StringAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTB3192730_13?cpp=>|vb=()|nu=[]");AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTB3192730_14?cpp=%"); This is an array whose values depend on the ItemType. If the ItemType is Load Pattern, this is the name of the load pattern. If the ItemType is Acceleration, this is UX, UY, UZ, RX, RY, or RZ, indicating the acceleration direction. If the ItemType is Link, this is the name of the link followed by U1, U2, U3, R1, R2, or R3 (in parenthesis), indicating the link degree of freedom for which the output is reported. If the ItemType is Panel Zone, this is the name of the joint to which the panel zone is assigned, followed by U1, U2, U3, R1, R2, or R3 (in parenthesis), indicating the degree of freedom for which the output is reported.

If this item is ObjectElm, the result request is for the point element corresponding to the point object specified by the Name item. If this item is Element, the result request is for the point element specified by the Name item. If this item is GroupElm, the result request is for all point elements directly or indirectly specified in the group specified by the Name item. params: Type: SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST9394533A_1?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");String The name of an existing link object, link element, or group of objects depending on the value of the ItemTypeElm item.

Type: ETABSv1AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST9394533A_2?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");eItemTypeElm This is one of the following items in the eItemTypeElm enumeration. If this item is ObjectElm, the result request is for the panel zone (link) element corresponding to the panel zone assignment to the point object specified by the Name item. If this item is Element, the result request is for the panel zone (link) element specified by the Name item. If this item is GroupElm, the result request is for all panel zone (link) elements directly or indirectly specified in the group specified by the Name item. If this item is SelectionElm, the result request is for all panel zone (link) elements directly or indirectly selected and the Name item is ignored. For GroupElm and SelectionElm a panel zone (link) element may be indirectly specified through point objects that have panel zone assignments.

methods.description: Reports the panel zone (link) forces at the point elements at the ends of the specified panel zone (link) elements
methods.signature: int PanelZoneForce( string Name, eItemTypeElm ItemTypeElm, ref int NumberResults, ref string[] Elm, ref string[] PointElm, ref string[] LoadCase, ref string[] StepType, ref double[] StepNum, ref double[] P, ref double[] V2, ref double[] V3, ref double[] T, ref double[] M2, ref double[] M3 )
params: Type: SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST2992F718_1?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");String The name of an existing point object, point element, or group of objects depending on the value of the ItemTypeElm item.

Type: ETABSv1AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST2992F718_2?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");eItemTypeElm This is one of the following items in the eItemTypeElm enumeration. If this item is ObjectElm, the result request is for the panel zone (link) element corresponding to the panel zone assignment to the point object specified by the Name item. If this item is Element, the result request is for the panel zone (link) element specified by the Name item. If this item is GroupElm, the result request is for all panel zone (link) elements directly or indirectly specified in the group specified by the Name item. If this item is SelectionElm, the result request is for all panel zone (link) elements directly or indirectly selected and the Name item is ignored. For GroupElm and SelectionElm a panel zone (link) element may be indirectly specified through point objects that have panel zone assignments.

methods.name: CreateAnalysisModel
methods.description:
Creates the analysis model. If the analysis model is already created 
 and current, nothing is done.

methods.return: Returns zero if the analysis model is successfully created or it already exists and is current, otherwise it returns a nonzero value.
methods.remarks: It is not necessary to call this function before running an analysis. The analysis model is automatically created, if necessary, when the model is run.

methods.name: MergeAnalysisResults
methods.description: Merges analysis results from a given model.
methods.signature: int MergeAnalysisResults( string SourceFileName )
params: Type: SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST80A9194C_1?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");String Full path to the model file to import results from.
methods.return: Returns zero if the results are successfully merged; otherwise it returns a nonzero value.
methods.remarks: The analysis model is automatically created as part of this function. See “Merging Analysis Results” section in program help file for requirements and limitations. IMPORTANT NOTE: Your model must have a file path defined before merging analysis results. If the model is opened from an existing file, a file path is defined. If the model is created from scratch, the File.Save function must be called with a file name before merging analysis results. Saving the file creates the file path.

methods.name: ModifyUndeformedGeometry

Modifies the undeformed geometry based on displacements obtained from

Type: SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST8DFA66A1_2?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");Double The scale factor applied to the displacements.
methods.return: Returns zero if it is successful; otherwise it returns a nonzero value

methods.name: ModifyUndeformedGeometryModeShape

Type: SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTC803A37_3?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");Double The maximum displacement to which the mode shape will be scaled
Type: SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTC803A37_4?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");Int32 The direction in which to apply the geometry modification XYZResultant
methods.return: Returns zero if it is successful; otherwise it returns a nonzero value

methods.name: RunAnalysis
methods.description: Runs the analysis.
methods.signature: int RunAnalysis()
methods.return: Returns zero if the analysis model is successfully run; otherwise it returns a nonzero value.
methods.remarks: The analysis model is automatically created as part of this function. IMPORTANT NOTE: Your model must have a file path defined before running the analysis. If the model is opened from an existing file, a file path is defined. If the model is created from scratch, the File.Save function must be called with a file name before running the analysis. Saving the file creates the file path.

methods.description: Retrieves auto seismic loading parameters for the 2016 ASCE 7 code
methods.signature: int GetASCE716( string Name, ref bool[] nDir, ref double Eccen, ref int PeriodFlag, ref int CtType, ref double UserT, ref bool UserZ, ref double TopZ, ref double BottomZ, ref double R, ref double Omega, ref double Cd, ref double I, ref double Ss, ref double S1, ref double TL, ref int SiteClass, ref double Fa, ref double Fv )
params: Type: SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST1FBBDA56_1?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");String The name of an existing Seismic-type load pattern with ASCE 7-16 auto seismic load assignment

Type: AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST1FBBDA56_2?cpp=array<");SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST1FBBDA56_3?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");BooleanAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST1FBBDA56_4?cpp=>|vb=()|nu=[]");AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST1FBBDA56_5?cpp=%"); This is an array with 6 inputs that indicates the seismic load directions nDir(1) = True = X DirnDir(2) = True = Y DirnDir(3) = True = X Dir + EccentricitynDir(4) = True = Y Dir + EccentricitynDir(5) = True = X Dir - EccentricitynDir(6) = True = Y Dir - Eccentricity
Type: SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST1FBBDA56_6?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");DoubleAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST1FBBDA56_7?cpp=%"); The eccentricity ratio that applies to all diaphragms

Type: SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST1FBBDA56_8?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");Int32AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST1FBBDA56_9?cpp=%"); This is 1, 2 or 3, indicating the time period option ApproximateProgram calculatedUser defined

Type: SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST1FBBDA56_10?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");Int32AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST1FBBDA56_11?cpp=%"); This is one of the following values. This item is used only when PeriodFlag is 1 or 2 0 = Ct = 0.028 (ft), x = 0.81 = Ct = 0.016 (ft), x = 0.92 = Ct = 0.03 (ft), x = 0.753 = Ct = 0.02 (ft), x = 0.75

Type: SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST1FBBDA56_16?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");DoubleAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST1FBBDA56_17?cpp=%"); The global Z-coordinate at the highest level where auto seismic loads are applied. [L]

Type: SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST1FBBDA56_18?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");DoubleAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST1FBBDA56_19?cpp=%"); The global Z-coordinate at the lowest level where auto seismic loads are applied. [L]

Type: SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST1FBBDA56_34?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");Int32AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST1FBBDA56_35?cpp=%"); This is 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 or 6, indicating the site class ABCDEF
methods.return: returns zero if the function is successfully retrieved; otherwise it returns a nonzero value

params: Type: SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_1?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");String The name of an existing static nonlinear staged load case

Type: SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_2?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");Int32AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_3?cpp=%"); The stage in the specified load case for which data is requested. Stages are numbered sequentially starting from 1
Type: SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_4?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");Int32AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_5?cpp=%"); The number of operations in the specified stage

Type: AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_6?cpp=array<");SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_7?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");Int32AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_8?cpp=>|vb=()|nu=[]");AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_9?cpp=%"); This is an array that includes 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, or 11, indicating an operation type. 1Add structure2Remove structure3Load objects if new4Load objects5Change section properties6Change section property modifiers7Change releases11Change section properties and age

Type: AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_10?cpp=array<");SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_11?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");StringAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_12?cpp=>|vb=()|nu=[]");AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_13?cpp=%"); This is an array that includes the object type associated with the specified operation. The object type may be one of the following: GroupFrameCableTendonAreaSolidLinkPoint The following list shows which object types are applicable to each operation type: Operation = 1 (Add structure)All object typesOperation = 2 (Remove structure)All object typesOperation = 3 (Load objects if new)All object typesOperation = 4 (Load objects)All object typesOperation = 5 (Change section properties)All object types except PointOperation = 6 (Change section property modifiers)Group, Frame, Cable, AreaOperation = 7 (Change releases)Group, FrameOperation = 11 (Change section properties and age)All object types except Point

Type: AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_14?cpp=array<");SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_15?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");StringAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_16?cpp=>|vb=()|nu=[]");AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_17?cpp=%"); This is an array that includes the name of the object associated with the specified operation. This is the name of a Group, Frame object, Cable object, Tendon object, Area object, Solid object, Link object or Point object, depending on the ObjectType item

Type: AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_18?cpp=array<");SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_19?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");Int32AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_20?cpp=>|vb=()|nu=[]");AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_21?cpp=%"); This is an array that includes the age of the added structure, at the time it is added, in days. This item applies only to operations with Operation = 1.

Type: AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_22?cpp=array<");SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_23?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");StringAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_24?cpp=>|vb=()|nu=[]");AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_25?cpp=%"); This is an array that includes a load type or an object type, depending on what is specified for the Operation item. This item applies only to operations with Operation = 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, or 11. When Operation = 3 or 4, this is an array that includes Load or Accel, indicating the load type of an added load. When Operation = 5 or 11, and the ObjectType item is Group, this is an array that includes Frame, Cable, Tendon, Area, Solid or Link, indicating the object type for which the section property is changed. When Operation = 6 and the ObjectType item is Group, this is an array that includes Frame, Cable or Area, indicating the object type for which the section property modifiers are changed. When Operation = 7 and the ObjectType item is Group, this is an array that includes Frame, indicating the object type for which the releases are changed. When Operation = 5, 6, 7, or 11, and the ObjectType item is not Group and not Point, this item is ignored and the type is picked up from the ObjectType item.

Type: AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_26?cpp=array<");SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_27?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");StringAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_28?cpp=>|vb=()|nu=[]");AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_29?cpp=%"); This is an array that includes a load assignment or an object name, depending on what is specified for the Operation item. This item applies only to operations with Operation = 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, or 11. When Operation = 3 or 4, this is an array that includes the name of the load assigned to the operation. If the associated MyType (load type) item is Load, this item is the name of a defined load pattern. If the associated MyType (load type) item is Accel, this item is UX, UY, UZ, RX, RY or RZ, indicating the direction of the load. When Operation = 5 or 11, this is the name of a Frame, Cable, Tendon, Area, Solid or Link object, depending on the object type specified. When Operation = 6, this is the name of a Frame, Cable or Area object, depending on the object type specified. When Operation = 7, this is the name of a Frame object.

Type: AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_30?cpp=array<");SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_31?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");DoubleAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_32?cpp=>|vb=()|nu=[]");AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST899863FF_33?cpp=%"); This is an array that includes the scale factor for the load assigned to the operation, if any. [L/s2] for Accel UX UY and UZ; otherwise unitless This item applies only to operations with Operation = 3 or 4.
methods.return: Returns zero if the data is successfully retrieved; otherwise, it returns a nonzero value

Type: AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTDC458461_14?cpp=array<");SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTDC458461_15?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");StringAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTDC458461_16?cpp=>|vb=()|nu=[]");AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTDC458461_17?cpp=%"); This is an array that includes the name of the object associated with the specified operation. This is the name of a Group, Frame object, Cable object, Tendon object, Area object, Solid object, Link object or Point object, depending on the ObjectType item

Type: AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTDC458461_18?cpp=array<");SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTDC458461_19?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");DoubleAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTDC458461_20?cpp=>|vb=()|nu=[]");AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTDC458461_21?cpp=%"); This is an array that includes the age of the added structure, at the time it is added, in days. This item applies only to operations with Operation = 1.

Type: AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTDC458461_22?cpp=array<");SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTDC458461_23?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");StringAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTDC458461_24?cpp=>|vb=()|nu=[]");AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTDC458461_25?cpp=%"); This is an array that includes a load type or an object type, depending on what is specified for the Operation item. This item applies only to operations with Operation = 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, or 11. When Operation = 3 or 4, this is an array that includes Load or Accel, indicating the load type of an added load. When Operation = 5 or 11, and the ObjectType item is Group, this is an array that includes Frame, Cable, Tendon, Area, Solid or Link, indicating the object type for which the section property is changed. When Operation = 6 and the ObjectType item is Group, this is an array that includes Frame, Cable or Area, indicating the object type for which the section property modifiers are changed. When Operation = 7 and the ObjectType item is Group, this is an array that includes Frame, indicating the object type for which the releases are changed. When Operation = 5, 6, 7, or 11, and the ObjectType item is not Group and not Point, this item is ignored and the type is picked up from the ObjectType item.

Type: AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTDC458461_26?cpp=array<");SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTDC458461_27?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");StringAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTDC458461_28?cpp=>|vb=()|nu=[]");AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTDC458461_29?cpp=%"); This is an array that includes a load assignment or an object name, depending on what is specified for the Operation item. This item applies only to operations with Operation = 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, or 11. When Operation = 3 or 4, this is an array that includes the name of the load assigned to the operation. If the associated MyType (load type) item is Load, this item is the name of a defined load pattern. If the associated MyType (load type) item is Accel, this item is UX, UY, UZ, RX, RY or RZ, indicating the direction of the load. When Operation = 5 or 11, this is the name of a Frame, Cable, Tendon, Area, Solid or Link object, depending on the object type specified. When Operation = 6, this is the name of a Frame, Cable or Area object, depending on the object type specified. When Operation = 7, this is the name of a Frame object.

Type: AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTDC458461_30?cpp=array<");SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTDC458461_31?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");DoubleAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTDC458461_32?cpp=>|vb=()|nu=[]");AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LSTDC458461_33?cpp=%"); This is an array that includes the scale factor for the load assigned to the operation, if any. [L/s2] for Accel UX UY and UZ; otherwise unitless This item applies only to operations with Operation = 3 or 4.
methods.return: Returns zero if the data is successfully retrieved; otherwise, it returns a nonzero value

methods.name: SetCaseList
methods.description:
Adds or modifies one load case or response combination in the list of cases 
 included in the load combination specified by the Name item.
methods.signature: int SetCaseList( string Name, ref eCNameType CNameType, string CName, double SF )
params: Type: SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST3DD12DC5_1?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");String The name of an existing load combination.

Type: ETABSv1AddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST3DD12DC5_2?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");eCNameTypeAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST3DD12DC5_3?cpp=%"); This is one of the following items in the eCNameType enumeration: LoadCase = 0LoadCombo = 1 This item indicates whether the CName item is an analysis case (LoadCase) or a load combination (LoadCombo).

Type: SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST3DD12DC5_4?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");String The name of the load case or load combination to be added to or modified in the combination specified by the Name item. If the load case or combination already exists in the combination specified by the Name item, the scale factor is modified as indicated by the SF item for that load case or combination. If the analysis case or combination does not exist in the combination specified by the Name item, it is added.
Type: SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST3DD12DC5_5?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");Double The scale factor multiplying the case or combination indicated by the CName item.
methods.return: Returns zero if the item is successfully added or modified, otherwise it returns a nonzero value.

Type: SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST6B7F1CE4_4?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");String The name of the load case or load combination to be added to or modified in the combination specified by the Name item. If the load case or combination already exists in the combination specified by the Name item, the scale factor is modified as indicated by the SF item for that load case or combination. If the analysis case or combination does not exist in the combination specified by the Name item, it is added.

Type: SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST6B7F1CE4_5?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");Int32 The mode number for the case indicated by the CName item. Any other case type or combo should specify a zero for this item.
Type: SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST6B7F1CE4_6?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");Double The scale factor multiplying the case or combination indicated by the CName item.

methods.name: GetInitialCase
methods.description: This function retrieves the initial condition assumed for the specified load case.
methods.signature: int GetInitialCase( string Name, ref string InitialCase )
params: Type: SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST55542BB3_1?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");String The name of an existing modal Eigen load case.

Type: SystemAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST55542BB3_2?cs=.|vb=.|cpp=::|nu=.|fs=.");StringAddLanguageSpecificTextSet("LST55542BB3_3?cpp=%"); This is blank, None, or the name of an existing analysis case. This item specifies if the load case starts from zero initial conditions, that is, an unstressed state, or if it starts using the stiffness that occurs at the end of a nonlinear static or nonlinear direct integration time history load case. If the specified initial case is a nonlinear static or nonlinear direct integration time history load case, the stiffness at the end of that case is used. If the initial case is anything else, zero initial conditions are assumed.
methods.return: Returns zero if the initial condition is successfully retrieved; otherwise it returns a nonzero value.
